subtopics.title: Projectiles and Trajectory
common_misconceptions: Horizontal motion affects vertical motion
Acceleration is zero at maximum height
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize independence of horizontal and vertical motions. Show that vertical acceleration is always g downward.

subtopics.title: Moments and Equilibrium
common_misconceptions: Equilibrium means no forces act
Center of gravity is always at the geometric center
subtopics.teaching_note: Use meter rules and known weights to verify the principle of moments. Show how to locate center of gravity experimentally.

subtopics.title: Resolution and Composition of Forces
common_misconceptions: The resultant is always larger than individual forces
Lami's theorem applies to any three forces
subtopics.teaching_note: Use force boards for experimental verification. Show that resultant can be smaller than components.

subtopics.title: Characteristics of SHM
common_misconceptions: SHM means motion that repeats
Acceleration is constant in SHM
subtopics.teaching_note: Use simple pendulum and mass-spring systems. Show that acceleration is proportional to displacement and directed toward equilibrium.

subtopics.title: Applications of SHM
common_misconceptions: Period of pendulum depends on mass
Amplitude affects the period significantly
subtopics.teaching_note: Derive period formulas. Show experimentally that period is independent of amplitude (for small angles) and mass for pendulum.

subtopics.title: Newton's Law of Gravitation
common_misconceptions: Gravity is the same everywhere on Earth
Mass and weight are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Distinguish mass (constant) from weight (varies with location). Use Cavendish experiment as historical context.

subtopics.title: Electric Fields and Charge
common_misconceptions: Positive charges have more charge than negative
Electric field and electric force are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Compare Coulomb's law with Newton's law of gravitation. Use field line patterns for different charge configurations.

subtopics.title: Capacitors
common_misconceptions: Capacitors store electric charge like a battery stores energy
Capacitance is the same as charge
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain that capacitors store energy in electric fields. Derive energy formula. Show combination formulas.

subtopics.title: Atomic Models and Structure
common_misconceptions: Electrons orbit like planets in exact paths
Rutherford's model was the final model
subtopics.teaching_note: Show how each model addressed limitations of the previous. Use spectral lines as evidence for energy levels.

subtopics.title: Nuclear Physics and Radioactivity
common_misconceptions: All radiation is harmful at any dose
Nuclear fission and fusion are the same process
subtopics.teaching_note: Discuss applications in medicine and energy. Explain safety measures. Use half-life calculations extensively.
